content.full_text:
Ravi Mehta
Chairman

Fair Political Practices Commission

June 12, 1995

Gina Verstrepen
San Joaquin County
Registrar of Voters
24 South Hunter Street, Room 202
Stockton, CA 95201

Re: Combined Campaign Reports
Our File No. I-95-191

Dear Ms. Verstrepen:

This is to confirm telephone assistance provided to you
regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act.¹ This letter constitutes written advice under Section
84205, which states:

The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit
candidates and committees to file campaign statements
combining statements and reports required to be filed by this
title.

A recall election has been called for Assembly District 17 to
be voted upon on August 22, 1995. Since the period for filing the
first pre-election statement covers only eight days after the
first semi-annual filing period, we believe it is appropriate for
committees primarily formed to support or oppose the recall, as
well as the officeholder who is the subject of the recall,
candidates being voted upon in the recall election, their
controlled committees, and committees which are primarily formed
to support or oppose those candidates, to file campaign disclosure
statements on the following schedule:

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seg. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800, Sacramento, California 95814 (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-95-191
Page 2


Type of Statement          Period Covered          Filing Deadline

1st Pre-Election          Jan. 1 - July 8          July 13

2nd Pre-Election          July 9 - Aug. 5          Aug. 10

Late Contribution;
  Late Independent
  Expenditure Reports     Aug. 6 - Aug. 21         Within 24 Hours

Semi-Annual              Aug. 6 - Dec. 31         January 31, 1996


     If you have additional questions, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Trish Mayer
                                        Political Reform Consultant
                                        Technical Assistance Division

cc:  Assemblyman Machado
     Secretary of State,
       Political Reform Division

sections.question_synthetic: What campaign disclosure filing schedule should be used for committees and candidates involved in a recall election for Assembly District 17 scheduled for August 22, 1995?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC permits committees formed to support or oppose the recall, the subject officeholder, recall candidates, their controlled committees, and supporting/opposing committees to file combined campaign disclosure statements according to a modified schedule with specific periods and deadlines for pre-election statements, late contribution reports, and semi-annual filings.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a procedural advice letter providing a filing schedule table. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present, but the purpose and FPPC response are clear. Document appears complete with recipient, date, and signature authority.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What campaign disclosure filing schedule should be used for committees and candidates involved in a recall election for Assembly District 17 scheduled for August 22, 1995?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC permits committees formed to support or oppose the recall, the subject officeholder, recall candidates, their controlled committees, and supporting/opposing committees to file combined campaign disclosure statements according to a modified schedule with specific periods and deadlines for pre-election statements, late contribution reports, and semi-annual filings.

A recall election has been called for Assembly District 17 to be voted upon on August 22, 1995. Since the period for filing the first pre-election statement covers only eight days after the first semi-annual filing period, we believe it is appropriate for committees primarily formed to support or oppose the recall, as well as the officeholder who is the subject of the recall, candidates being voted upon in the recall election, their controlled committees, and committees which are primarily formed to support or oppose those candidates, to file campaign disclosure statements on the following schedule: 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seg. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800, Sacramento, California 95814 (916) 322-5660 Our File No. A-95-191 Page 2 Type of Statement Period Covered Filing Deadline 1st Pre-Election Jan. 1 - July 8 July 13 2nd Pre-Election July 9 - Aug. 5 Aug. 10 Late Contribution; Late Independent Expenditure Reports Aug. 6 - Aug. 21 Within 24 Hours Semi-Annual Aug. 6 - Dec. 31 January 31, 1996 If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. Sincerely, [signature] Trish Mayer Political Reform Consultant Technical Assistance Division cc: Assemblyman Machado Secretary of State, Political Reform Division
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming telephone assistance regarding combined campaign report filing under Section 84205 for the Assembly District 17 recall election, providing a specific filing schedule with adjusted deadlines for various types of campaign disclosure statements.
